sections.title: 1. Purpose
sections.section_prompt: State that this assessment evaluates cybersecurity risks for {device_name_model} per FDA Premarket Cybersecurity Guidance (2023), AAMI TIR57, and EU MDR Annex I 17.2. Risk-control measures feed back into {ref:RD-007} and software requirements {ref:SW-002}.

sections.title: 2. Framework
clauses: 2.3 Vulnerability assessment (SOUP CVEs, static/dynamic analysis, penetration test)
2.4 Risk estimation using CVSS v3.1 base + environmental
2.5 Risk control: secure design, defense in depth, secure update, logging
2.6 Post-market vulnerability monitoring and coordinated disclosure
sections.section_prompt: For each clause, describe how it is executed for {device_name_model}. Reference SBOM source and update cadence.

sections.title: 3. Threat & Vulnerability Register
sections.ai_rows_prompt: Generate 10–16 threats for {device_name_model}. Cover STRIDE categories. Include at least 2 SOUP-CVE threats referencing the SOUP register in {ref:SW-003}. CVSS as decimal 0.0–10.0; residual must be ≤ base. Controls realistic (TLS 1.3, mTLS, RBAC, signed updates, secure boot, input validation, rate limiting, audit log). Return JSON array.

sections.title: 4. SOUP CVE Monitoring
sections.section_prompt: Describe how SBOM is maintained, how new CVEs against SOUP components from {ref:SW-003} are detected (NVD feed, Dependabot, supplier bulletins), triaged within an SLA (e.g. critical within 48h), and patched via {ref:SW-010} configuration management and {ref:HI-009} change control.

sections.title: 5. Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
sections.section_prompt: Describe the coordinated vulnerability disclosure policy for {company_legal_name}, including contact channel, acknowledgement SLA, fix timeline, and customer notification through {ref:CA-006} FSCA when applicable.

output_config.footer: CONFIDENTIAL — Controlled Document | FDA / EU MDR Cybersecurity
